scenario.context: Station 5 of the daily run: secure addressing and client setup.
scenario.mission: You check MAC/IP roles, core services, and a clean client setup in the company network.

station.profile: DHCP, DNS, gateway, subnet, and standardized client addressing

customerRequest.subject: Station 05: check client addressing and setup
customerRequest.body: Please check the basic configuration for new clients. Focus on MAC and IP, DHCP, DNS, gateway, plus a short explanation of the standard setup.

options.text: The MAC address belongs to the interface, the IP address places the device logically in the network
options.rationale: This distinguishes the two address types well in daily work.

options.text: MAC and IP always have the same task
options.rationale: Both address types serve different purposes.

options.text: The IP address replaces any network card
options.rationale: Without an interface, the address cannot be used.

feedback.correct: Correct: MAC and IP are clearly separate addressing concepts.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: separate physical interface and logical assignment.

questions.title: Assign DHCP, DNS, and gateway
questions.prompt: Which statements are technically correct? (Multiple selections)

options.text: DHCP can automatically provide clients with network parameters
options.rationale: This is a typical standard for workstation systems.

options.text: DNS translates names into usable address information
options.rationale: Without DNS, many services are impractical for users.

options.text: The default gateway is used for destinations outside the local network
The MAC address replaces the subnet mask
Static addresses should only be assigned on request and documented
options.rationale: This reduces conflicts and administrative errors.

feedback.correct: Correct: You assign the core services cleanly.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: separate DHCP, DNS, gateway, and documentation requirements more clearly.

questions.title: Check chain for a new client setup
questions.prompt: Arrange the steps for a clean setup.
items: Verify IP configuration incl. DNS and gateway
Check name resolution and central services
correctOrder: Verify IP configuration incl. DNS and gateway
Check name resolution and central services

feedback.correct: Correct: This checks the setup systematically and traceably.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: first connection and parameters, then services and documentation.

questions.title: Usable host addresses in a /26 network
questions.prompt: How many usable host addresses does a /26 subnet provide?

feedback.wrong: Determine host bits and subtract network and broadcast addresses.

questions.title: Explain the standard setup
questions.prompt: Explain in 4-6 sentences why standard clients usually use DHCP and what role DNS and gateway play.
questions.expectedOutline: Explain standardization, reachability, and service usage in short sentences.

feedback.correct: Correct: The setup is understandable and technically clean.
feedback.wrong: Please explain DHCP, DNS, and gateway and how they work together more clearly.
